title: On Courage, History & the Strength of Women
excerpt: March 8 is not only a celebration. It is a reminder of courage, dignity, and the power that emerges when women support one another.
content: Today, many people see March 8 as a day of flowers and congratulations. But the story of this day began very differently.

At the beginning of the 20th century, women in many countries had no right to vote, limited access to education, and almost no voice in political life. Their work was often difficult and largely unrecognized.

In 1910, at the International Conference of Working Women in Copenhagen, German activist Clara Zetkin proposed creating a day that would remind the world of the struggle for women’s rights. Her idea was simple: one day each year that would unite women from different countries, cultures, and professions.

Just a year later, the day was observed for the first time in several European countries. Then, in 1917, women in Petrograd took to the streets demanding “Bread and Peace.” Their protest became one of the events that helped change the course of history. That is how March 8 became established as International Women’s Day.
More than a century has passed since then. During this time, women gained the right to vote, to study, to lead companies, to work in science, to create art, and to shape economies and societies.
But perhaps the most important change is this: women’s voices are now heard.

Today, millions of women around the world build businesses, raise families, lead innovations, create culture, and shape the future. Each of them is part of this story — a story of courage and of moving forward.
That is why March 8 is not simply a celebration. It is a reminder. A reminder of courage. Of dignity. Of the right to be yourself.
And of the truth that when women support one another, a powerful force emerges — one that can change the world.
Because together, we are a great force.
And today I would like to say something to every woman, wherever she may be — in a large city or a small town, on a busy street or in a quiet room.
May you always have the courage to walk your own path.
May you be surrounded by people who recognize your worth.
May your dreams never feel too big.
May your life truly belong to you.
And may you always remember that you are never alone.
Happy International Women’s Day to women everywhere.
